title: English Training
description: Turn English into a skill you actually use!

scenes.speech: Welcome to the English Dojo! Get the key to connect with the world! 🌍
scenes.title: English Dojo
scenes.content: In English training, what matters more than memorization is how often you use the language. Train reading, listening, speaking, and writing in rotation, and growth comes faster.

scenes.title: Self-Intro Training
scenes.content: Be ready to say your favorites, your daily routine, and your future dream in English. Then any conversation can start. Order that lands clearly matters more than perfect grammar.
scenes.highlight:
3 things to share:
- What you love
- Your daily life
- Your dream

scenes.speech: Listening grows when you pair 'subtitles + sound'!
scenes.title: Listening Training
scenes.content: Watch a favorite clip with native subtitles, then English subtitles, then no subtitles. Same content, three rounds of training for your ears. Start with short videos.
scenes.highlight:
3-step listening:
1. Native subs
2. English subs
3. No subs

scenes.speech: Practicing with AI is great too — no shyness needed!
scenes.title: AI Conversation
scenes.content: Tell ChatGPT or Claude 'be my casual English partner', and you have a practice room as long as you want. No one judges, so speak out loud and make mistakes freely.
scenes.highlight:
AI tips:
- Pick a topic
- 5 minutes a day is fine
- Ask for corrections

scenes.speech: Save what you learn into a 'short notes' phrase book!
scenes.title: Phrase Notebook
scenes.content: Words are easier to remember when stored by situation rather than alone. Collect five each for 'thanking', 'asking', and 'sharing your view' to make them reusable.
scenes.highlight:
Scene examples:
- Thanking
- Requesting
- Stating opinion

scenes.title: 30-Second Speech
scenes.content: A 30-second limit forces you to keep only what truly matters. Record it and listen back — you'll catch your own pronunciation habits and ideas to fix.

scenes.speech: Five minutes a day is enough! Consistency wins!
scenes.title: How to Keep Going
scenes.content: Five minutes daily beats two hours once. Don't chase perfection — stack 'I touched it today'. Three months later you won't recognize your old self.
scenes.highlight:
Keep-going tips:
- Fix the time
- Start tiny
- Track it

scenes.speech: Great work! Let's speak out to the world! 🌟
scenes.title: Training Complete!
scenes.content: English isn't for tests; it's a tool to talk with friends across the world. Record today's 30-second speech so your future self can listen back.
